page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is an entry-level general-purpose virtual machine based on the x86_64 architecture. It features a single dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU operating at 2.3 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory, which yields 3.75 GB of memory per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 45 MB of L3 cache. It supports hardware-level features such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), AES cryptography, and Bit Manipulation Instructions (BMI2), though it lacks AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This instance type does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations position this instance in the lower performance tiers. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng, yields a score of 1088.79, which falls into the bottom decile of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show 80.25 GB/sec for cached read operations, dropping to 10.68 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 9.71 GB/sec for uncached writes. Geekbench testing reflects these constraints, with a single-core score of 895.0 and a multi-core score of 898.0. Cryptographic and database operations also register in the lower percentiles, with Redis SET operations achieving 1.29 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 1.21 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-standard-1 offers a low-cost entry point for basic cloud hosting, but delivers low resource density and limited computational throughput per resource unit. Due to its single-core limitation and poor multi-threaded performance, it is unsuitable for intensive workloads like data science, large-scale LLM inference, or heavy database operations. It is best utilized for lightweight, non-critical tasks such as basic web serving, simple scripting, development environments, or low-traffic microservices that do not require high memory bandwidth or parallel processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is an entry-level, general-purpose virtual machine featuring a single dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU running at 2.3 GHz and 4.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the lower percentiles, with a Geekbench single-core score of 895.0 and a multi-core score of 898.0. Memory bandwidth is limited, reaching 10.68 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Given these hardware constraints, the n1-standard-1 provides a highly economical baseline for workloads with minimal resource demands. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, and simple background scripting tasks that do not require high-performance parallel processing or extensive memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is an entry-level general-purpose instance with 1 Intel Xeon vCPU and 4.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for lightweight workloads, basic web serving, and development environments.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-1 is a 1 vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM general-purpose instance designed for lightweight workloads and basic web serving.
tagline: An entry-level, single-core virtual machine with 4 GB of memory, designed for basic web serving and lightweight development tasks.

4.0 GB of system memory with no local storage or GPU acceleration
Low-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance in benchmark testing
Cost-effective baseline for non-production and development environments
Best suited for lightweight microservices, basic web serving, and simple scripting
